opps.title: Application Development and Support Services - FMCS
opps.description:
The General Services Administration, Office of Centralized Acquisition Services, on behalf of the Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service, is issuing this Request for Quote to establish a single-award Blanket Purchase Agreement under GSA Multiple Award Schedule SIN 54151S Information Technology Professional Services.

FMCS requires Application Development and Support Services to maintain, support, modernize, secure, integrate, and enhance its enterprise application environment. The FMCS environment includes Microsoft 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Platform, Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power BI, Dataverse, LMS365, Azure Government / GCC Medium, custom .NET/C# applications, SQL Server, PostgreSQL where applicable, Collabspace Enterprise Records Management, CCMS, Arbitration systems, the FMCS public web environment, AWS-hosted workloads, and legacy systems.

The scope includes project management, operations and maintenance, minor enhancements, major enhancements, application development, modernization and migration support, cloud migration support, cybersecurity compliance support, ATO/POA&M support, Section 508/accessibility support, records management support, documentation, reporting, quality control, transition support, and related technical services.

The BPA will include the following service CLINs:

CLIN 0001 Project Management
CLIN 0002 Operations and Maintenance and Minor Enhancements
CLIN 0003 Major Enhancements, Modernization, and Migration
The Government anticipates issuing Initial BPA Call 0001 concurrently with BPA award for Base Period ADSS Services. The anticipated BPA period of performance consists of one 12-month base period and three 12-month option periods. The estimated BPA ceiling is $4,500,000. The BPA does not obligate funds; funds will be obligated only through BPA Calls issued by the Contracting Officer.

This RFQ is a 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside under GSA MAS SIN 54151S. Only quotes submitted by GSA Sch

opps.title: Video Production Professional Staffing
opps.description:
The Department of State's Bureau of Global Public Affairs (GPA), Office of Video (GPA/DC/VID), is soliciting proposals for Digital Video Staffing Services (DVDS) in support of its mission to communicate U.S. foreign policy priorities to domestic and global audiences. The contractor shall provide a professional staff of approximately 25-50 personnel to conceive, produce, and deliver high-quality video, broadcast, and live interactive web content across digital and traditional media platforms worldwide. Services span the full production lifecycle from pre-production planning and scripting to post-production editing and global live broadcast origination and must be executed in an agile, responsive manner consistent with the fast-paced demands of a news-style environment.

The scope of work encompasses three primary task areas: (1) Professional Digital Media Production, including social media video, motion graphics, and documentary-style content; (2) Digital Broadcast, Interactive, and Live Streaming, including studio operations, live coverage of Department press briefings, and Secretary-level engagements; and (3) Video Broadcast Infrastructure Maintenance and Technology Services, including procurement, maintenance, and support of broadcast and IT equipment and software subscriptions. Contractor personnel must be willing to travel domestically and internationally on short notice, work nights and weekends as required, and maintain constant readiness to support time-sensitive tasking. Performance will be conducted primarily at the Department's headquarters at 2201 C Street, NW, Washington, D.C.

Please see all attachments included with this RFP for full details, technical requirements, performance standards, and contractual specifications governing this acquisition. Offerors are responsible for reviewing all attached documents prior to submitting a proposal.

All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officers at HofhineAK@st

opps.title: Integrating Technology into the Delivery of Clinical Care at Health Centers
opps.description:
The purpose of this initiative is to advance the integration of technology, digital innovation (through AI and patient wearable devices) to support more efficient chronic disease prevention and control, with a focus on hypertension, diabetes, and obesity, across HRSA-funded health centers and to reduce health center administrative burden. 
Wearable devices, including smartwatches, rings, fitness trackers, and continuous glucose monitors (CGMs), generate continuous patient health data that, when securely integrated into electronic health record (EHR) systems, equip providers with timely, actionable insights to improve clinical decision-making, close gaps in care, and support value-based reimbursement. 
This contract will also incorporate sister agency initiatives, where applicable, including the Center for Medicare & Medicaid Service's (CMS's) Advancing Chronic Care with Effective Scalable Solutions (ACCESS), Food and Drug Administration's (FDA's) Technology-Enabled Meaningful Patient Outcomes (TEMPO) for Digital Health Devices Pilot, and the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology (ONC) interoperability standards, including the United States Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) and USCDI+ guidance to complement the five pillars of the HHS AI Strategy.
By improving digital and health literacy for both patients and providers, this initiative centers on a patient-focused approach; empowering patients, strengthening shared decision-making, and aligning with HHS's Making America Healthy Again (MAHA) initiative. Through tailored technical assistance, peer learning, and evaluation, this program will help health centers implement strategies that measurably improve outcomes for patients and communities across the country.

opps.title: FTCA Compliant Risk Management Certification Program
opps.description:
The purpose of this requirement is to enable HRSA to fulfill its statutory obligations under 42 U.S.C. 233 by strengthening patient safety, risk management, and operational consistency across FTCA-deemed health centers through a standardized, nationally recognized training and certification program leveraging an existing solution tailored to HRSA needs. This effort will establish clear and enforceable competency benchmarks for risk management professionals, ensuring alignment with FTCA requirements and federal expectations for oversight, accountability, and risk mitigation.
The program will promote consistency in risk management practices, including documentation, credentialing oversight, and claims analysis, improving the defensibility of clinical care and reducing avoidable malpractice exposure to the Federal Government. By equipping staff with the necessary knowledge and skills, it will enhance compliance, strengthen risk identification and mitigation, and support a culture of patient safety, ultimately standardizing risk management capabilities and reducing variability across the Health Center Program.
To establish and deliver a comprehensive, standardized healthcare risk management training and certification solution that leverages an existing, nationally recognized certification program, tailored to meet HRSA and FTCA requirements, and equips health center and free clinic staff in ambulatory care settings with the knowledge and proficiency to effectively manage risk. This initiative supports HRSA's statutory responsibilities under 42 U.S.C. 233 by building core competencies to enable participants to lead, implement, and sustain risk management programs; improving consistency in documentation, credentialing oversight, and risk analysis; and strengthening compliance with FTCA requirements. The program will address inconsistencies in current practices, enhance patient safety, and reduce federal malpractice exposure by improving the defensibility of care. It will

opps.title: Avaya PBX Phone System Replacement for Glacier National Park
opps.description:
Glacier National Park (GLAC) currently operates two legacy Avaya/Nortel CS1000 Private Branch Exchange (PBX) systems interconnected via T1 trunks between the Park Headquarters in West Glacier and the Saint Mary Visitor Center complex. The existing infrastructure supports approximately 300 IP/VoIP endpoints (Avaya 1120E handsets), 150 analog lines (serving fax, alarm, and OPX circuits extending up to 24 miles), and voicemail services for approximately 400 mailboxes.

The current CS1000 platform has reached end-of-life (EOL) and end-of-support (EOS) status, so the purpose of this requirement is to procure and install an Avaya IP Office platform at the Headquarters and Saint Mary locations, including 300 Avaya J179 IP phone handsets, software licensing, auto attendant/phone tree and voicemail configuration, 3-year maintenance support, and professional installation services.

This IS a brand name requirement.

This IS a total small business set-aside.

Contract Type: The contract awarded as a result of this RFQ will be a firm-fixed price commercial contract.

Questions are due at least 5 BUSINESS DAYS before the quote due date, making them due by COB 7/21/2026 at the latest.

NOTE: Your company must also be registered in System for Award Management (SAM) at the time of award and throughout the entire award/contract performance - The registration must be "active" in SAM.gov. - Government will award to the technically acceptable offer from a responsible offeror, that represents the best value to the government

*** BE ADVISED - THE PRICE IN GSA eBUY WILL BE THE EVALUATED PRICE ***
CONTRACTING POC: Kiara Stewart - kiara_stewart@nps.gov

The goal of this contract is to procure and install a new Avaya IP Office platform to ensure continued reliable communication services at both the Headquarters in West Glacier and the Saint Mary Visitor Center complex.

opps.title: Ohio National Guard Marketing & Advertising Services
opps.description:
The Ohio Army National Guard (OHARNG) is seeking sources for marketing and advertising services for their Recruiting & Retention Battalion (RRB). The objective of this requirement is to establish a contractor-supported digital marketing and lead management ecosystem that enables the OHARNG RRB to modernize recruiting operations by consolidating online recruiting presence, implementing call tracking and routing visibility, replacing Excel-based lead tracking with a CRM, coordinating paid media administration, delivering quarterly market research, automating cold lead nurturing, and providing performance reporting for Government oversight.

The purpose of this announcement is to gain knowledge of potential sources for these services including interested Small Business sources. This notice is for planning purposes only. All interested parties should respond. The Ohio National Guard intends to issue a solicitation and award of a contract for these services.

All interested concerns will provide this office, in writing, with a notice stating their positive intent to submit a proposal no later than July 27th, 2026. This notification, which shall not exceed five typewritten pages, must include (1) positive statement of intent to bid as a prime contractor, and (2) A listing of similar services provided over the past three years, both for government and private industry. The type of service, dollar value, Contract number, location, and Point of Contact are to be included. 

In the event adequate Small Business contractors are not available for adequate competition for this project, it may be advertised as unrestricted. Information shall be provided via email to:
Primary: Claudia Tenaglia, Contracting Specialist, claudia.j.tenaglia.civ@army.mil
Alternate: Herbert Brown, Contracting Officer, herbert.l.brown24.civ@army.mil

Include the following in the subject line "Request for Information (RFI)# 1823475. No facsimile responses will be honored.

opps.title: Common API Services (CAS)
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization provides enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities that enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems. These capabilities reduce duplication, lower risk and cost, and accelerate delivery of modernization initiatives.

The D&PE API Services organization requires contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain mission-critical middleware applications and shared services that support tax processing, taxpayer services, and other IRS mission-essential functions.

The objective of the Performance Work Statement (PWS) is to:

-	Modernize legacy middleware platforms, including webMethods Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), Bulk Request Management Gateway (BRMG), and Business Rules Engine (BRE), into microservices-based architectures using Spring Boot and OpenShift standards 
-	Maintain uninterrupted operations during transition 
-	Modernize common business services (APIs) and sustain existing services throughout the modernization efforts
-	Upgrade applications to current Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) versions and/or OpenShift (Enterprise Container Platform (ECP)

The IRS requires full lifecycle support (analysis, development, testing, and deployment) for applications hosted in IRS on-premise environments. This support includes delivery of software releases aligned to IRS Forms processing, modernization initiatives, and legislative requirements.
opps.summary:

 The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), through its Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization, aims to provide enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities. This initiative is designed to enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems, reducing duplication and lowering costs while accelerating modernization efforts.

The D&PE API Services organization seeks contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain critical middleware applications and shared services

opps.title: Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering Organization requires contractor support to modernize, automate, operate, and sustain the Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).

Under this effort, the Contractor shall provide full lifecycle technical services, including design, development, configuration, modernization, operations, and maintenance of the Enterprise API Gateway environment. 

An Application Programming Interface (API) is a defined set of rules and protocols that enables different software systems to communicate and exchange data, and an API gateway is a centralized service that manages, secures, routes, and monitors API traffic between providers and consumers. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of operations during the transition and maintain support for existing APIs while implementing modernization improvements.

The end products of this contract will include a modernized Enterprise API Gateway operating model which consists of:

-	Automated API onboarding and Continuous Integration /Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines
-	Enterprise API governance artifacts and standards
-	Security controls aligned with Zero Trust principles
-	Developer enablement documentation and tools
-	Integration with various Artificial Intelligence (AI) providers, consumers, and/or gateways
-	Sustained production operations and performance monitoring for the duration of the contract
The results of this contract will be used to improve system interoperability, standardize API lifecycle management, enhance cybersecurity, and support IRS modernization and mission delivery requirements.

opps.title: Financial Management and Business Support Services
opps.description:
Sources Sought Notice - #2032H326N00011
Financial Management and Business Support Services

1.0	SYNOPSIS
The Treasury Common Services Center (TCSC) provides shared acquisition and operational support services across the Department of the Treasury (Treasury). As part of this mission, TCSC Acquisition Services supports Treasury-wide procurement reporting, analytics, data quality, and acquisition oversight activities.

The U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of the Deputy Chief Financial Officer (ODCFO) requires specialized financial analysis, accounting, and business services to support its business operations.  These services will help improve operational efficiencies, systems and data integrity, and support the implementation of new and current agency-wide business initiatives. Responses to this Sources Sought Notice (SSN) will help ODCFO assess the marketplace for contractors capable of delivering the required services.

The contractor shall provide financial management and accounting support services to include technical research of GAAP, OMB, and legislative requirements, accounting and analytical services in areas such as proprietary and budgetary reporting, variance analysis, problem resolution, data reconciliation, abnormal accounting conditions and business process improvements while also helping to maintain robust internal controls that facilitate Treasury's compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and guidance.  The Contractor must provide all management, labor, and supervision necessary to satisfy Performance Work Statement (PWS) requirements.

The Treasury has not determined the acquisition approach. Any results of this SSN will assist the Treasury Departmental Offices and the Departmental Offices & Enterprise Procurement Branch, TCSC Acquisition Services in determining an acquisition strategy about how to proceed.

This is NOT a formal solicitation or request for proposals, proposal abstracts, or quotes. The purpose of this notice is to obtain i

opps.title: A Comparison of Health Care Use and Mortality among Dual Eligibles
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration
opps.description:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office of the Assistant Secretary for Planning and Evaluation (ASPE), Office of Mission Acquisition Solutions (OMAS) has a requirement for: "A Comparison of Health Care Use and Mortality Among Dual Eligibles in C-SNPs and I-SNPs to Those in Other Medicare Advantage Plans" in accordance with Section C Program Description and Background.  

The Contractor shall independently provide all personnel, labor, facilities, materials, equipment, supplies, and services necessary to perform the Statement of Work, except those expressly identified as Government-furnished.

The study will conduct two quantitative analyses: the first comparing outcomes for dually eligible individuals in Chronic Condition Special Needs Plans (C-SNPs) to those in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and regular Medicare Advantage (MA) plans; the second comparing outcomes for those in Institutional Special Needs Plans (I-SNPs) to those in D-SNPs and regular MA plans. 

The work includes project management, data acquisition through Data Use Agreements (DUAs), development of a detailed work plan and data analysis plan, rigorous data analysis using various government data sources, and the production of research briefs and presentations suitable for policy audiences. 

The Federal Supply Schedule Category "Professional Services," Special Item Number: 541611 and the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code: 541611 "Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services" are applicable to this requirement.  The Product Service Code is: R799 (Other Management Support Services).

opps.title: Dorm Wifi Service
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial items prepared in accordance with the format in FAR Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; proposals are being requested and a written solicitation will not be issued.

This is a non-personnel services contract to provide Wireless internet services to the permanent party dorm campus on Little Rock AFB. The Government shall 
not exercise any supervision or control over the contract service providers performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government.

The contractor shall provide a comprehensive, turnkey, managed bulk Wi-Fi internet service for military personnel residing in dormitory buildings. The purpose of this service is to deliver reliable, high-speed wireless internet access free of charge to the end-users. The solution must cover all designated dorm rooms and common areas, ensuring consistent performance and positive user experience. The contractor shall be responsible for all aspects of service delivery, including network management, security, customer support, and ongoing maintenance.

Please review all attached documents.

- Network management and security

opps.title: Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC)
opps.agency: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
opps.description: The Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC) program is a CMS initiative designed to safeguard the integrity and accuracy of payments made to Medicare Advantage (MA) plans. As the primary mechanism through which CMS validates payment accuracy, the RADV program relies on this contractor to perform and support a comprehensive suite of medical record review activities. These activities include the intake of medical records, abstraction of diagnosis codes, quality assurance and continuous improvement efforts, appeals support, and systems and reporting enhancements all of which work together to ensure that MA plan payments are based on accurate and verified clinical documentation. This task order is issued under the Center for Program Integrity (CPI) Payment Accuracy Acquisition Strategy, reflecting CMS's broader commitment to program integrity and responsible stewardship of federal healthcare funds.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
After the completion of the State Laws digest on illegal passing, a more in-depth review or state of knowledge report is needed to determine if a model law for illegal passing would help with the consistency of enforcement of these driving violations. More clarity if needed on how currently laws are enforced to help better inform states to improve enforcement of these laws. The state laws digest informed that laws vary in content, organization, consistency, and clarity. Also, that what may constitute illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neigh-boring one. States also vary in their methods to review, document and report violations including permissibility of stop-arm cameras. This report is needed to give clarity on how states can best improve enforcement of this law.

To address this need, this project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This study will provide if a model law would be necessary for continuity among enforcement within the U.S States and provide best example of a model law.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is seeking to enhance the enforcement of laws against the illegal passing of school buses, a significant safety concern for students. 

The project aims to address inconsistencies in enforcement and prosecution across various jurisdictions, as current laws vary widely in their content and clarity. A previous digest indicated that illegal passing violations are often dismissed in court, highlighting the need for improved education and advocacy among judicial officials. opps.agency: Office of the Secretary of the Interior
opps.description:
The U.S. Department of the Interior, Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement (OSMRE), seeks quotations from GSA MAS contractors for continued Operations & Maintenance (O&M), ETL/GIS workflow support, system enhancements, legislative/policy update integration, cybersecurity compliance, and transition planning for the Enhanced Abandoned Mine Land Inventory System (eAMLIS).
eAMLIS is a mature, mission-critical system supporting statutory obligations under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act (SMCRA). It provides annually reported AML inventory information to DOI leadership, Congress, States, Tribes, and external stakeholders. System requirements, technical objectives, and operational responsibilities are detailed in the attached Statement of Objectives (SOO).

The Contractor shall provide all personnel, tools, equipment, and services necessary to maintain stable system operations, support ETL pipelines and geospatial features, execute modernization efforts, and ensure timely updates in response to legislative changes and federal audits (including OIG recommendations).

A hybrid Firm-Fixed-Price (FFP) and Labor-Hour (LH) order is anticipated.

The Government intends to award a task order from this solicitation to the responsible offeror whose offer conforming to the solicitation will be most advantageous to the Government, price and other factors considered. The following factors shall be used to evaluate offers: 
Factor 1: Technical Approach (Most Important)
Factor 2: Relevant Experience & Past Performance
Factor 3: Price

The Government will select the best-suited vendor using a comparative evaluation process IAW FAR 80405-2(c)(3). 

Please see attached solicitation (B06 - SOL-eAMLIS) for full RFQ details.

It does not constitute a Request for Quotation (RFQ) or a promise to issue an RFQ in the future. This request for information does not commit the Government to contract for any supply or service whatsoever. The Government will not reimburse any costs incurred by vendors in the preparation of information submitted in response to this notice.

1. Introduction & Purpose
The New Mexico Air National Guard (NMANG) is seeking to identify businesses, particularly small businesses, capable of providing a comprehensive sports marketing and sponsorship package. The purpose of this sponsorship is to improve the visibility and community footprint of the NMANG, increase brand awareness, generate qualified recruiting leads, and promote military service opportunities.

The Government is seeking to determine if there are capable small businesses that can perform the required work, specifically through a partnership with the New Mexico Goatheads sports franchise or the Rio Rancho Events Center. The information received will assist the Contracting Officer in determining the appropriate acquisition strategy, which may include a small business set-aside competition.

To effectively support its recruiting mission, the NMANG requires access to high-visibility sporting events that attract a diverse and large-scale audience throughout New Mexico. A strategic sponsorship with a premier sports franchise offers continuous exposure to thousands of spectators through venue advertising, digital media, promotional events, and fan engagement opportunities. The place of performance will be the Rio Rancho Events Center, located at 3001 Civic Center Circle, Rio Rancho, NM 87144, during New Mexico Goatheads home games and associated events.

The contractor shall be required to provide a comprehensive sponsorship package that includes, but is not limited

opps.title: International Procurement Experts
opps.description:
The Millennium Challenge Corporation (MCC), Program Acquisition & Assistance Practice Group (PAA) within the Department of Compact Operations (DCO) seeks to award contract with a firm to provide multiple procurement experts on an as-needed basis with extensive international procurement experience under donor funded projects, such as funded by the World Bank, MCC, Asian Development Bank etc. These experts will support MCC PAA in developing procurement policy, guidance, and tools for use by MCC partner country Accountable Entities (MCAs) in their procurement operations and assist MCC PAA in measuring performance of those operations and recommending improvements in procedures and application of tools. The Contractor will assist MCC PAA in guiding the MCAs and their procurement agents through providing training, advice, hands-on support on MCC's procurement policies, procedures, guidance and standard bidding documents, procurement-related technologies, market outreach efforts, and helping them in overseeing the compliance thereto to meet MCC requirements. Extensive interaction with MCC will be required for the experts to fully understand MCC's procurement documents and/or to advise MCC in modifying these documents as and when needed.

See Section M of the attached solicitation for evaluation criteria.

opps.title: Network Engineers and Specialists Support (NE&S)
opps.description:
Non-personal services to provide network sustainment and deployment support services to the Military Health System (MHS) Medical Treatment Facilities (MTFs) both inside and outside the continental United States (CONUS and OCONUS). This work includes functions such as network performance
measurement and monitoring, assistance with network design and development, network measurement, customer service in the form of trouble tracking and troubleshooting at the Tier 0 level, onsite support for both contractor and Government support teams for escalated trouble tickets, as well as maintenance and sparing support. Such services will be provided in accordance with stated MHS and specific Medical Component priorities. Services shall also comply with existing and evolving technical architecture guidance from international, commercial, DoD, and Health Affairs (HA) sources.  These services will be provided in a centralized and decentralized mode. The on-site network specialists will be the primary 24/7 on-call decentralized points of contact within the MTFs. Additional support and guidance will be provided by regional network engineers and DHA centralized infrastructure team.

opps.description: PLEASE NOTE: This procurement will be procured under the 8(a) program and only businesses currently enrolled in the 8(a) program shall be considered. The Naval Surface Warfare Center, Carderock Division (NSWCCD) has a requirement to maintain the current Common Access Card (CAC) support office on site at the West Bethesda, MD location. The CAC support office allows for all military, civilian, and contractor personnel working on site, or for other DoD/Government agencies, to obtain and maintain their CACs. GSA Wants Your Feedback to Enhance Domestic Preference in GSA Acquisition Programs - Market Research

In accordance with Executive Order 14392 "Ensuring Truthful Advertising of Products Claiming to be Made in America" and in order to advance the Administration's policy to revitalize American manufacturing and promote American workers, the General Services Administration (GSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI). The RFI seeks input from industry stakeholders on how to regenerate an important part of the American economy by making it easier to buy American products through GSA Advantage. Only responses submitted through the RFI link below will be accepted. Responses submitted through any other means will NOT be evaluated or recorded.

RFI Link: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_cvEH8Pm0kAwRYfY

(NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the link into a new browser.)

Additional Information:
Your company may receive multiple RFI notifications for this requirement since it is being posted on GSA eBuy, SAM.gov. and marketed widely on GSA platforms. Your company only needs to complete the RFI survey one time.

Any questions regarding this RFI can be sent to: rfi@research.gsa.gov. Please include the RFI number in your email title.

opps.title: 19RC1126Q0004 MEDIA MONITORING SERVICE FOR DAMASCUS-SYRIA
opps.description:
The Contractor shall provide daily, comprehensive media and social media monitoring, analysis, reporting, and dissemination services to support U.S. Government objectives and interests in Syria. The Contractor will deliver one comprehensive report per day that summarizes key narratives, provides in-depth coverage of political, social, and economic developments, and highlights emerging trends and shifts in media tone. The report shall also include monitoring and analysis of social media platforms to identify trending topics, influential actors, tonality of discussions, and message tracking relevant to Syria.

This service is critical to ensure U.S. policymakers and embassy officials receive timely, reliable, and actionable insights for informed decision-making and strategic planning in a rapidly evolving information environment.

 The Contractor will monitor a broad spectrum of media sources including, but not limited to:


	Syrian local news outlets (print, online, television, and radio).
	Syrian social media platforms (Twitter/X, Facebook, Instagram, Telegram, YouTube, TikTok, and other platforms relevant to the Syrian information space).
	Regional and international media outlets with significant coverage or influence on Syrian affairs.


The monitoring and analysis must provide U.S. Government officials with a clear understanding of the evolving media environment in Syria, identifying key narratives, trends, influencers, and disinformation campaigns impacting U.S. policy interests.

The Contractor shall provide daily, comprehensive media and social media monitoring, analysis, reporting, and dissemination services to support U.S. Government objectives and interests in Syria. The Contractor will deliver one comprehensive report per day that summarizes key narratives, provides in-depth coverage of political, social, and economic developments, and highlights emerging trends and shifts in media tone. The report shall also include monitoring and analysis of social media

opps.title: ILS Aerospace Parts Marketplace Subscription
opps.description:
INTRODUCTION

The Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division (NAWC-AD) intends to solicit and award a sole source Firm Fixed Price (FFP) purchase order to Inventory Locator service, LLC (ILS) for web-based global inventory query subscriptions for locating and purchasing aftermarket aerospace parts that are unavailable through the federal supply service and/or have long lead times from approved U.S. Government vendors. Request for Quote (RFP) Number N0042126Q1175 will be issued on a sole source basis with ILS under the authority implemented by the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 12 and 13. The Period of Performance is anticipated to be a  one year  base period with four one-year option periods.  

This notice of intent is not a request for competitive quotes. However, all responses to this notice of intent must be received no later than 12:00 pm (EST) on 21 July 2026 to Taylor Wade at taylor.a.wade7.civ@us.navy.mil.

A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed contract upon the response to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government. Information received will normally be considered for the purpose of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement. The Government will not pay for any information received.

Contractor must be registered in System for Award Management (SAM) Database prior to the submission of an offer to be considered for award of any Department of War Contract. This can be accomplished electronically at https://sam.gov/content/entity-registration.

Please provide the following information with your response:

1. Company Name:

2. CAGE Code:

3. DUNS #

4. Payment Terms of NET 30.

5. Pricing – Are these items on a commercial published, on-line, or internal price list.

6. If available on a price list, provide a copy. All price listings will be kept confidential.

7. If not available on a price list please provide information on how pricing is determined (Example: item cost + %, labor rate a

The Defense Health Agency Contracting Activity (DHACA) in coordination with the The Defense Health Agency Global Emerging Infections Surveillance program office (DHA GEIS) intends to negotiate on a sole source basis, with BAO Systems, 2900 K St NW Suite 506 Washington, DC 20007  as the only responsible source that can provide hosting services for DHIS2, support services, and annual end-user training.

The Government requires 


	Hosting Services (DHIS2 and “Learn Moodle”): set up, hosting and maintenance of DHIS2 software on a designated number of servers, system monitoring and alerts, BAO Manager console access, system and infrastructure recommendations and guidance
	Support Services: Service hours for ad hoc backups, configuration guidance, application and database troubleshooting, database importing, end user support, , troubleshooting upgrades, version recommendations


Annual Training: virtual training on relevant administrative functions, data entry, analytic processes, and/or data export.

BAO Systems is the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) for DHIS2. It specializes in DHIS2 implementation, hosting, and support services and offers a comprehensive suite of tools which enhances system stability, performance, and security. It is currently the only U.S. based company with an active SAM registration that offers fully managed, scalable, cloud-based hosting for DHIS2 with 24/7 monitoring and support, ensuring high availability, real-time data aggregation, and advanced security features. It is also the only known DHIS2 Platform with a focus on multi-country deployments, custom applications, and systems integration to meet diverse organizational needs.

opps.title: Facility Inspection & Detainee Death Review Services
opps.description:
The Office of Professional Responsibility (OPR) is an organizational element within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The Office of Detention Oversight (ODO) is a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office (ISP). ODO conducts compliance inspections of detention facilities in which detainees are housed for more than seventy-two (72) hours to determine compliance with the ICE 2025 and 2019 National Detention Standards (NDS), the 2025 Non-Dedicated Intergovernmental Detention Standards (NDIDS), the 2011 Performance Based National Detention Standards (PBNDS), as applicable, and the 2020 Family Residential Standards (FRS).
The External Reviews and Analysis Unit (ERAU) is also a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office. ERAU is responsible for conducting administrative reviews of all deaths of individuals in ICE custody. The Detainee Death Review (DDR) is a focused, fact finding, objective review of the facts and circumstances surrounding a detainee's death. ERAU also conducts special and focused reviews, as requested by ICE leadership.
The Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ), on behalf of ICE/ERAU is seeking a vendor who shall provide support services related to inspections and detainee death reviews to determine facility compliance with the NDS, NDIDS, PBNDS, or FRS, and for supporting designated analytical activities within ODO and ERAU.  The vendor will provide expert operational, analytical and administrative support for ODO and ERAU inspections, reviews, and special assignments.
The vendor shall provide productivity and management support, including but not limited to, quality control; data input; clerical support; statistical and analytical documentation and data manipulation; training of personnel; and other related functions. The vendor's project managers for both inspections and DDRs shall perform day to day management of overall contract support operations and ensure conformity with all contract

These inspections assess compliance with various detention standards, including the ICE National Detention Standards (NDS) and Family Residential Standards (FRS).

opps.title: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access planform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components.
opps.description: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access platform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components. The Contractor shall perform research of repair line-item costs in each Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) code areas across the country to provide a database that represents the local economy in a disaster location. Services specified under this contract require quarterly updates and additional pricing work that reflects changes in prices due to current economic conditions in each FIPS code location. Develop and update the FEMA Line-Item Database in a Microsoft Access format with an interactive front-end application capable of entering a disaster number, selecting a state or territory, selecting counties, municipalities or parishes within the state or territory, and exporting the pricing data in FEMAâ€™s designated tab delimited format described in Attachment B. 3.1.1. The line-item format includes a category, line-item number, line-item name, unit of measure and a description of repair represented by the line item. The line-item cost is a sum of all cost components associated in the use of that line item. The database shall be updated quarterly, delivered to FEMA through an FTP (File Transfer Protocol) site to be imported by FEMA. The first deliverable of the base and each option year shall be delivered in the first week of: March=Q1, June=Q2, September=Q3, December=Q4. Develop an annual Line-Item Mapping Master â€“ A Microsoft Excel file of the Line-Item basis and rationale used for pricing that provides a breakdown of the line-item components as CSI codes with national average costs.

The goal of this requirement is to continue to maintain this commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) product (Case Management Development Platform powered by Entellitrak) in order to provide a system capable of ensuring the agency is within regulatory compliance. Similar products will be implemented for other capabilities such as Anti-Harassment (AH). This COTS solution will continue to be hosted on Military Cloud (milCloud) development, test and production environments. Additional responsibility for this effort will be to provide operations, sustainment and engineering support as well as system updates and maintenance of all non-production environments.

opps.title: IFMS Services & Support
opps.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
opps.description:
The purpose of this contract is to provide maintenance and support for Information Technology System Services to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) Office of Safety Security and Asset Management (OSSAM) for the Integrated Facilities Management System (IFMS). This includes sustainability of the overall system, its customized modules and custom integration applications and programs as well as revising, testing, documenting, operating and maintaining the Facilities Management Information (FMI) parts of IFMS.

The assigned components of the Government's Integrated Facilities Management System will be maintained and enhanced as necessary to support OSSAM operations, operability and coordination among various TRIRIGA and non-TRIRIGA software applications.
CDC and end users will be provided high quality training and support.

Please see attached RFQ for additional information. Questions should be received at jpowell5@cdc.gov NLT June 10, 2026 at 3:00 p.m.EST. Quotes are due to be received via GSA E-Buy no later than July 30, 2026 at 3:00 p.m. EST

opps.title: 89th Administrative Security Support Services
opps.description:
The contractor will be required to provide all personnel and expertise required to perform comprehensive administrative, personnel, and physical security support to the 89th Attack Squadron (89 ATKS) at Ellsworth Air Force Base, South Dakota. The selected Contractor will independently execute and manage all designated functional security areas to ensure complete operational coverage and regulatory compliance with applicable DoD, AF, and Intelligence Community Directives (ICDs). Ultimately, the objective is to maintain continuous, secure operations within the squadron's footprint by managing all collateral and SCI security programs required for assigned personnel to execute their combat and training missions.

**Critical Eligibility Notes: Vendors MUST possess an active, verifiable Top Secret Facility Clearance at the time of proposal submission. Additionally, proposed personnel MUST possess active, adjudicated TS/SCI clearances eligible for performance on Day 1.**
The Period of Performance: This effort includes a 1-year Base Period, (4) 1-year option periods, and a 6-month optional extension.


Questions are to be sent to the Contract Administrator and OCO at the email addresses listed in this RFP not later than 12:00pm MST, 15 July 2026. Please reference the solicitation number and request title in the subject line of all email inquiries.
Proposals are to be sent to the Contract Administrator and OCO at the email addresses listed in this RFP not later than 12:00pm MST, 30 July 2026.

opps.title: Serial Subscription Services
opps.description:
Brookhaven Science Associates (BSA) is seeking industry feedback regarding the potential acquisition of comprehensive serial subscription services. The anticipated requirement is for a contractor to provide all necessary qualified personnel, facilities, systems, and resources to manage the placement and administration of new and renewal subscriptions for domestic and international serials, periodicals, online databases, technical reports, and other print and electronic information resources.

The contractor would be responsible for providing end-to-end subscription management services, including processing new and renewal orders, maintaining subscription records, coordinating with publishers, resolving claims and delivery issues, providing customer support, and performing all activities necessary to ensure uninterrupted access to subscribed resources. Services shall support subscriptions delivered to single or multiple locations and encompass both print and electronic formats.

opps.title: 165 ASOS HPO Services
opps.description:
Special Warfare (SW)/Tactical Air Control Party (TACP) in the Air National Guard (ANG) must always be ready to perform at their peak in order to accomplish their challenging mission set.  Attaining and maintaining this level of peak human performance requires the implementation of a top-notch physical conditioning and maintenance program comparable to those seen in Olympic-level and professional sports organizations.  The Human Performance Optimization (HPO) program will focus on the whole operator concept to not only assist TACP operators in accomplishing their military missions in the short term, but also to emphasize the maintenance of peak performance over the long term in order to lengthen the service life of the "human weapon system" while ensuring high-quality production.  The contractor shall provide all personnel, equipment, tools, materials, supervision, and quality control necessary, except as specified in Paragraph 3.0 as Government Furnished, to perform Preservation of the Force and Family Services, as defined in this PWS.

Please Note: 
Review all attachments in their entirety prior to submitting a proposal.  To be considered for award, you must complete and sign the SF 1449.  

A Site Visit will not be conducted for this acquisition.

Any and all questions are required to be submitted No Later Than close of business 21 July 2026.  Answers will be provided no later than close of business 28 July 2026.

Please submit all questions to:  justin.smith.173@us.af.mil and andrew.headrick@us.af.mil.

This program aims to ensure that TACP operators can perform at peak levels, similar to professional athletes, by providing comprehensive physical conditioning and maintenance services. The goal is to support both immediate military mission requirements and long-term health and performance sustainability.

opps.title: Base Telecommunications Systems Operation and Maintenance Services
opps.description:
COMBINED SYNOPSIS/SOLICITATION FOR COMMERCIAL ITEMS
Joint Base Charleston (JB CHS) Base Telecommunications Systems Operations and Maintenance (BTS O&M) Services
RFQ FA441826Q0053

Amendment 01 
The purpose of this amendment is to:
1.	Alter the non-mandatory site visit date from 21 Jul 2026 to 29 Jul 2026 at 0900 AM EDT. All prospective offerors interested in attending the site visit shall submit a completed Base Access Form 111 (Atch 11) and a copy of a Real ID (front and back) for each contractor personnel to Madison Nerenberg, Contract Specialist, at madison.nerenberg@us.af.mil and Taylor McDaniel, Contracting Officer, at taylor.mcdaniel.1@us.af.mil by Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 at 1200 PM EDT if they plan to attend.
2.	Alter the due date for Questions from 23 July 2026 to 31 Jul 2026.
3.	Alter the date Answers will be posted from 28 July 2026 to 04 Aug 2026.
4.	All other terms and conditions remain unchanged. 
	  
(i) This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products or commercial services prepared in accordance with RFO part 8 and 12. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. Offers are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.

Solicitation number FA441826Q0053 is issued as a Request for Quotation (RFQ) for Joint Base Charleston (JB CHS) Base Telecommunications Systems Operations and Maintenance (BTS O&M) Services. 

This acquisition is 100% small business set-aside for GSA MAS 54151S holders only. The applicable Product Service Code is DG01 - IT and Telecom - Network Support Services (Labor) and NAICS code is 517111 - Wired Telecommunications Carriers for this acquisition. The size standard for the appliable NAICS is 1,500 employees. 

This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov and selecting FAR Overhaul.

This is not a Defense Priorities and Allocations System (DPAS) rated acquisition.

(ii)

opps.title: Weather Stream MicroWave Sounder Data Buy
opps.description: This acquisition seeks access to acquire commercial, near-real-time MWS data, including optional hyperspectral observations, from on-orbit MWS systems. The focus is on Low Earth Orbit (LEO) observations suitable for operational data assimilation, short-term forecast improvement, tropical cyclone monitoring, precipitation-related applications, and environmental hazard assessment. Depending on sensor design, MWS may also provide imagery and atmospheric vertical wind profiles. The capabilities described in Section II.B and II.C  will provide NOAA with scalable and flexible access to commercial MW data, enhancing forecast accuracy, situational awareness, and NOAA’s role as the Nation’s authoritative source of environmental intelligence.

opps.title: This procurement is for access to the comprehensive law library via UHD Hard Disk with a site license for unlimited use in support of Naval Consolidated Brig Miramar.
opps.agency: NAVSUP Fleet Logistics Center San Diego
opps.description:
Notice of Intent to Sole Source

THIS IS A NOTICE OF INTENT TO AWARD A SOLE SOURCE CONTRACT AND IS NOT A REQUEST FOR COMPETITIVE QUOTES. Reference: Solicitation Number N00244-22-Q-0245. Naval Supply Systems Command (NAVSUP) Fleet Logistics Center San Diego (FLCSD) intends to award a firm fixed price contract under the authority of FAR 6.302-1(a)(2)(i)(A), only one source being available to meet the Government’s needs, to:

 RELX, Inc. - DBA: LexisNexis
 9443 Springboro Pike 
 Miamisburg, OH 45342-4425  

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this requirement is 511199; the business size standard is 500 (number of employees).

The objective of this sole source award is to issue a Firm Fixed-Price (FFP) contract for LexisNexis UHD, consisting of a multitude of legal resources, in support of the Naval Consolidated Brig Miramar (NAVCONBRIG Miramar).
SECNAVISNT 1640.9C and American Correctional Association standards requires our command to provide comprehensive resources for legal research on Military and Federal Law. Military Correctional Facility Miramar does not allow prisoners internet access so this is the only and or least expensive option. The UHD (Hard Disk) allows prisoners to search a multitude of legal databases on their time and without the requirement of staff supervision. If this system were not used, it would require many man hours to supervise each prisoner requiring access to legal materials. Additionally, the prisoners would not have ready use of legal materials, which may preclude them from meeting certain deadlines for clemency or trial.
The UHD is provided by Lexis Nexis with a site license for unlimited use by prisoners.

RELX, Inc. DBA: LexisNexis is the standard all Legal offices use and is the only electronic law library service available on a hard disk drive because prisoners cannot have internet. Only RELX was identified that offered this solution. The only other law library services available are onli

opps.description:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, VA Boston Healthcare System, Center for Information Dissemination and Education Resources (CIDER) intends to negotiate on a sole source basis, pursuant to FAR 6.302-1, with the Society for Academic Emergency Medicine (SAEM) for scientific journal supplement publication services. 

CIDER requires services to produce a special journal supplement of peer-reviewed manuscripts in the Academic Emergency Medicine (AEM) Journal of Society for Academic Emergency Medicine (SAEM). The manuscripts written by VA Health Service Research and Development Service (HSR&D) and invited investigators/respondents will be focused on the  state of the art of emergency care in the VA healthcare system.  AEM/SAEM is uniquely qualified due to its emphasis on clinical trials, observational cohort studies, and economic or policy research, all of which are a part of health services research; its ranking as a top journal in the field; and its target audience and membership cohort of academic emergency physicians to produce a special journal supplement.
 
This notice of intent is not a request for competitive proposals; however, any responsible source who believes it is capable of meeting the requirement may submit a capability statement to the contracting office no later than Wednesday, February 23, 2022, 3:00 PM, EST.  Interest/capability statements may be sent to Shannon Hukriede at Shannon.Hukriede@va.gov. No telephone responses will be accepted. A determination not to compete the proposed requirement based upon the responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

opps.title: Department of Commerce (DOC) Cloud Services Provider Vehicle Strategy
opps.description:
1. Notice of Strategy

The Department of Commerce (DOC) is providing this notice to inform industry of its procurement strategy for the Cloud Vehicle Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA). This 10-year vehicle, with an estimated value of $4.105 Billion, is designed to provide native hyperscale cloud services across the Department.

2. Direct-to-CSP Approach

Due to highly specialized technical requirements, including massive compute elasticity (25,000+ concurrent vCPUs), proprietary 100+ Tbps global backbones, and specific hardware density for AI/ML and weather modeling, the Department is utilizing a Direct-to-CSP strategy. It is contemplated that to ensure mission success and direct engineering support, only Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) acting as the Cloud Service Provider (CSP) will be eligible for award.

3. Authority

This strategy is being executed via GSA eBuy under the authority of GSAR Subpart 538.71. Participation is restricted to authorized contractors holding a GSA Multiple Award Schedule (MAS) under SIN 518210C.

4. No Action Required

This notice is for informational purposes only to ensure transparency in the Department's long-term cloud acquisition planning. The Department is not seeking quotes, proposals, or feedback as part of this notice.

5. Points of Contact

Eric Olmstead, Contracting Officer: eric.olmstead@noaa.gov

opps.title: Sources Sought - DCMA Blue List Program Support Services
opps.description:
This is a sources sought posting to confirm the capabilities of GSA OASIS contractors to meet our Government requirement. NOTICE: This is NOT a solicitation, and no award will be made as a result of this Sources Sought Posting. 

The Government requirement is to hire 11 Full-Time Employees (FTE) in the following areas: One (1) Task Lead, two (2) Project Managers, two (2) IT Project Managers, two (2) IT Cybersecurity Specialists, one (1) Management Analyst, two (2) Contract Administrators, and one (1) Public Affairs/Media Outreach Specialist for one base year and one option year. This effort supports the agency's bona fide need to secure the sUAS supply chain, accelerate procurement timelines, and eliminate administrative barriers for warfighters seeking to acquire secure drone technology.

Attached is a DRAFT Performance Work Statement for industry review, and the Government is looking for feedback and any information that can help with creating an official solicitation. We are anticipating to release the official Request for Proposal in August, with award in September. Please provide any questions, feedback, or any insights possible to help make this possible. 

- How feasible are the requirements laid out in the Performance Work Statement (PWS) attached?
- How quickly, upon hiring, could you hire, onboard, and relocate (as applicable) to meet FTE needs?

opps.title: Managed Attribution Systems (Chameleon)
opps.description:
The Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) Cyber Support Section (ODV) needs to continue to modernize and streamline DEA’s Internet-based investigative resources with an integrated managed attribution and evidence collection system. Deployment of these capabilities, across the entire DEA will equip agents and analysts with standardized technical tools necessary to safely and effectively conduct online research and collect and preserve evidence.

Conducting Internet-based investigations and research browsing has multiplied over the past several years. With the advent of Drug Trafficking Organizations (DTOs) relying heavily upon Internet-based applications and Dark Web sites to facilitate their criminal enterprise, it is anticipated that in the future, electronic communications will be the preferred and the exclusive means of communication. The threat landscape described creates opportunities for law enforcement to exploit illicit Dark Web market sites utilized by DTOs and dismantle their operations.

opps.title: Anti-Terrorism Prevention Support
opps.description:
The Virgin Islands National Guard (VING) requires contracted Anti-Terrorism (AT) support to sustain its force protection readiness and ensure compliance with DoD, Army, and National Guard Bureau (NGB) directives. These services are essential for safeguarding personnel, infrastructure, and information across all VING facilities and operational areas. The contractor will be responsible for updating AT policies and plans, conducting vulnerability assessments, managing Random Antiterrorism Measures (RAM), facilitating training, maintaining reporting tools and systems, coordinating with local and federal law enforcement, and supporting operational security and emergency response efforts.

This support is critical to meet regulatory requirements set forth in AR 525-13, CNGBI 0700.01, and related AT doctrine. Additionally, the contractor must support inspections, ensure training compliance, maintain readiness during elevated threat conditions, and deliver monthly reports. The contract will include one base year and four 12-month option periods, with a provision for a six-month extension in accordance with FAR 52.217-8.

The Financial Services Industry of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Large Business and International Division (LB&I) employees Revenue Agents who examine complex financial transactions engaged in by taxpayers. Financial Services Agents examine tax issues that are national in scope, significant in dollars, important to tax administration and/or important for uniform and consistent treatment across the IRS. LB&I International Examiners, Engineers and Subject Matter Experts also assist in the examination of taxpayers’ financial transactions.

Financial transactions include, but are not limited to, stocks, bonds, mortgages, foreign currency, commodities, options, forward contracts, futures contracts, swaps, and other financial derivatives. As part of the examination of a financial transaction, the Revenue Agent must understand and analyze the market price and financial background of financial instruments. Although some financial data, such as the prices of publicly traded domestic stock, is publicly available on the internet, financial data for many financial instruments can only be obtained by accessing private, subscription-only financial services

The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) plans to award a contract without competition to Premier Healthcare Solutions, Inc.

The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services’(HHS), Office of the Assistant Secretary for Health, through the Office on Women’s Health and the Office of Minority Health, works to improve health and well-being nationwide. These offices do this by providing leadership, coordinating programs, delivering effective services, and promoting evidence-based policies and education, with a focus on women, girls, and underserved populations.

Acquisition Services

Action

Notice of Intent to Award a Sole Source Indefinite Delivery / Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) Contract

Description

The U.S. Department of the Treasury, Departmental Offices & Enterprise Procurement Branch, Treasury Common Services Center - Acquisition Services, intends to award a sole source contract to Pitchbook Data Inc. for a subscription to a commercial-off-the-shelf research database that provides the Treasury Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) with the ability to:


	Research companies and investors in private capital markets.
	Access detailed data related to private credit markets, including leveraged loans and related news and coverage of the full lifecycle of loans.
	Search investments made by specific actors and countries.
	Look at specific sectors of investment (semi-conductors, 5G, biotech, etc.).
	Build overall acumen for investment trends and provide actionable research.
	Receive up-to-date information on transactions that may be overlooked by the news/press.


The Government has determined that Pitchbook Data Inc. is the only source capable of meeting the Government's requirements because PitchBook Data Inc. developed and owns the propriety and patented hardware, software, and algorithms that are used in the required products and support the services specifically required for this acquisition.

The anticipated award is for a base period of 12-months, with four (4) option period(s), if applicable, from September 1, 2026 through August 31, 2031. Task Orders placed against the proposed IDIQ will have their own periods of performance.

The USDA Enterprise Depot is partnered and integrated with 3rd party vendors to assist with overflow storage and shipping services.

opps.title: Ginnie Mae Financial Systems & Information Technology & Modernization Support
opps.description:
Ginnie Mae requires a Contractor to provide financial support services for its internal financial management and accounting operations. These services include support for the management of OCFO financial systems and the provision of secure access to those systems. The Contractor's overall support shall align with Ginnie Mae's mission and meet the functional requirements defined by the Federal Systems Integration Office and Ginnie Mae's Office of the Chief Financial Officer. Support shall advance modernization across the following areas: Program Management; Systems Operations and Maintenance; Systems Assessment and Modernization; Systems Enhancements; Project-Specific Enhancements and Assessments; Systems Infrastructure Collaboration; Application Provisioning; Security and IT Compliance; and Systems Training.

All quotes during Phase I must be submitted through the GSA eBuy web portal at: https://www.ebuy.gsa.gov. Quotes submitted by any other method may not be considered. This acquisition will use an advisory down-select, phased evaluation and source-selection process. The procurement will consist of a two-phase response and evaluation approach and in accordance with the RFQ.

Questions regarding Phase I of this solicitation must be submitted no later than July 20, 2026 at 10 a.m. EST. See RFQ Section 11.4.1 and 11.4.2 for additional details.

Phase I quotes must be submitted through the GSA eBuy web portal no later than July 28, 2026 at 10:00 a.m EST.

NOTE: The Government is accepting questions regarding Phase I only.

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source- West Virginia High School Football Statewide Broadcast Sponsorship
opps.description:
In accordance with RFO FAR 5.101, this is a public notification of a proposed sole-source contract action. The West Virginia Army National Guard (WVARNG) Purchasing and Contracting Office intends to solicit and award on a sole-source basis under the authority of RFO FAR 12.102(a) and FAR 13.101(b) to West Virginia Radio Corporation (dba MetroNews Sports).

Requirement:

The WVARNG Recruiting and Retention Battalion has a requirement for a comprehensive, statewide high school football broadcast sponsorship. The requirement consists of a one (1) year base period and two (2) one-year option periods. Deliverables include synchronized statewide radio broadcast integration across 47 stations, exclusive streaming and mobile app placements, and presenting sponsorship rights for the season-opening kickoff game.

Justification for Sole Source:

The West Virginia Secondary School Activities Commission (WVSSAC) is the governing body for high school athletics and holds the exclusive intellectual property and media rights for high school championship events. WVSSAC has granted exclusive broadcasting and media syndication rights to MetroNews Sports. As the sole proprietor of these exclusive legal rights and the only network with a 47-station synchronized footprint covering all 55 counties in West Virginia, MetroNews Sports is the only vendor capable of fulfilling the WVARNG's requirement for statewide broadcast integration and live in-game commercial placement.

Disclaimer:

This Notice of Intent is not a request for competitive proposals or a solicitation for offers. A solicitation will not be issued. However, any vendor that believes it can meet the requirements as outlined above may submit a capability statement or proposal, which shall be considered by the agency.

Capability statements must be submitted in writing via email to keisha.l.george.civ@army.mil no later than July 21, 2026 0700AM EST. The capability statement must provide clear, irrefutable evidence of the vendo

opps.title: R408--MAIL MANAGEMENT SERVICES ANSWERING VENDOR QUESTIONS
opps.description:
Q1. Please confirm that the VBA receives 41,000 packets per day on average but the ask of the contractor for this TO is to process up to 5-7,000 after the third month.  The potential is more as the contractor proves its value.  This 5-7,000 is all USPS mail and mail that is being shipped to contractor by VBA RO's.
A1. The Government confirms that VBA estimates receiving approximately 41,000 mail packets per day across all centralized mail operations; however, this figure represents the total mail volume processed through the full centralized mail pipeline and is not the contractor's Task 5.14 obligation. Task 5.14 Mail Packet Processing Services is a distinct function separate from mail receipt, scanning, and conversion.  It represents human-assisted claims processing actions, analogous to work a claims assistant would perform, on mail packets that automated processing was unable to complete, ensuring mail is actioned the same day it enters a work queue. The contractor's Task 5.14 obligation is to process 5,000 7,000 packets per day following the Month 3 ramp-up period as outlined in Table 5 of PWS Section 5.14.  The Government notes that this volume has trended downward over time as mail automation capabilities have matured, though temporary spikes may occur due to automation issues or special projects.

opps.title: Invasive Carp Regional Coordinating Committee -support services
opps.description:
This is a sources sought posting for the following support services:


Project Scope: 
The purpose of this project is to support ongoing efforts of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (FWS) and U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to convene the bi-national, 26-member Invasive Carp Regional Coordinating Committee (ICRCC) for the purpose of assisting ICRCC members in implementing their authorities to reduce and/or eliminate the threats posed by invasive carp to the Great Lakes. The USFWS and EPA serve as co-chairs of the ICRCC. This project includes support for development of the annual ICRCC Invasive Carp Action Plan. Support activities will be conducted at the direction of the EPA and FWS as the U.S. federal agency co-chairs of the ICRCC. The purpose of the ICRCC and the annual Action Plan is to assist ICRCC members in implementing their authorities to reduce and/or eliminate the threats posed by invasive carp to the Great Lakes. 

Project Description:
This project provides key staffing to support the effective operation of the ICRCC, under the leadership of the FWS and EPA as co-chairs. Due to the technical nature and time sensitiveness of tasks, experience with coordinating and managing invasive carp reporting and meetings in necessary. Efforts are to include but not limited to:

Ongoing activities to support operations and coordination of the ICRCC:
-	Meet virtually with the ICRCC co-chairs on regularly scheduled Teams calls twice weekly (or additional, as needed) to discuss the on-going, time-sensitive activities of the ICRCC. May include pre-meeting agenda development and keeping track of action items.
-	Support convening once-per-month (or as needed) ICRCC Teams platform video teleconferences for interagency discussion of relevant issues/updates on invasive carp management focused on supporting Great Lakes protection from invasive carp, and work with the co-chairs and member agencies to develop and distribute meeting materials and agendas. 
-	Support con

opps.title: REQUEST FOR INFORMATION -LOC Novel High-Density Optical Data Storage
opps.description:
This is a Request for Information (RFI). The Library of Congress (LOC) reserves the right to cancel this RFI and any corresponding solicitation issued in the future. This RFI shall not be construed in any way to be a solicitation and is for informational purposes only.

The following is a general description of the requirements the LOC is seeking responses from the industry on:

The Library of Congress, hereinafter referred to as the “Library” or “LOC”,” is exploring innovative preservation methods for its digital collections. The LOC is seeking to determine the feasibility of utilizing ultra-high density optical data storage designed for the long-term preservation of a subset of the Library’s digital collections. Currently, the Library manages over 1 billion unique objects of digital preservation data (~33 petabytes) via traditional storage methods. For longevity and reduced migration intervals, it may be reasonable for this portion of data to be permanently stored on a novel medium. The focus would be on a solution that exhibits high areal density, a long lifespan, reduced maintenance/migration cycles, is immutable, and displays a high level of durability. The Library aims to demonstrate the feasibility of an innovative optical data storage solution as a high-density, scalable, and durable medium for storing its preservation collection.

The Library wishes to examine the feasibility of this method on a small subset of its digital collections. To be successful, a variety of data formats—including video, audio, text, and images— must be encoded onto the media, errors must be detected/recorded, preservation techniques must be validated and select data must be recalled/read with high fidelity. This work will contribute to the boundary pushing field of high-density optical data storage while pushing the boundaries of access and preservation.

Please see the full document 'LOC Novel High Density Optical Data Storage RF

The goal is to find a solution that offers high areal density, long lifespan, reduced maintenance/migration cycles, immutability, and durability. rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.

opps.title: Electrical Law Library (ELL)
opps.description:
This is a pre-solicitation notice of a proposed contract action issued in accordance with part 12 – Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services. This is not a solicitation or invitation for offerors to submit bids.

The Federal Bureau of Prisons (FBOP), located at 320 First St. NW, Washington DC, anticipates issuance of request for quotation (RFQ) 15BPCC26Q00000020 for a single award firm-fixed price (FFP) definitive contract for electronic legal research to be utilized all Bureau of Prisons institutions throughout the United States.

The performance period will include a one-year base followed by one option period with an Option to Extend Services FAR 52.217-8.

This acquisition is not set-aside for small business concerns. This will be solicited open market on an unrestricted basis. The NAICS code for this requirement is 519290 – Web Search Portals and All Other Information Services and PSC Code R605 – Library Services.

A solicitation will be issued on or about March 19, 2026, and will be posted to SAM.gov, with an estimated closing date of March 26, 2026. Hard copies of the solicitation will not be made available.  The website provides download instructions.  Any secure/locked documents (if applicable) uploaded with the solicitation will require an active Sam.gov vendor registration for access.  If you encounter problems accessing these documents, please contact the Federal Service Desk at 866-606-8220.  All future information about this solicitation, including any amendments, will also be distributed solely through this website.  Interested parties are responsible for continuously monitoring this website to ensure that they have the most recent information about this proposed contract action.

IMPORTANT: Any contractor interested in obtaining a contract award with the Federal Bureau of Prisons must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) at www.sam.gov. All offerors are urged to double check their SAM registration

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source: ForeFlight Aeronautical Navigation Flight Charts
opps.agency: CBP Office of Information and Technology
opps.description:
Notice of Intent to Sole Source: U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) intends to award a sole source Firm-Fixed Price (FFP) contract to Jeppesen ForeFlight, Inc. of applications for aeronautical navigation flight charts (ForeFlight – Military Flight Bag Performance). This award will be in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulations (FAR) Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) 12.102(a). These items are only available from the original equipment manufacturer (OEM), Jeppesen ForeFlight, Inc (formerly Boeing Digital Solutions).

This application will be utilized by CBP Air and Marine Operations (AMO) pilots for domestic operations (expect unmanned aircraft systems). 

The ForeFlight – Military Flight Bag Performance offers comprehensive and total coverage of the Continental United States (CONUS) providing flight planning, charts, weather details, airport information, and other necessary aviation tools that are critical for flight safety and operation. No other applications currently meet the flight navigation requirements expect for this proposed item.

The anticipated contract period of performance will be from September 20, 2026 through September 19, 2027.

This notice is neither a request for proposal nor an invitation for bids. There is no formal solicitation available for this requirement at this time. If responsible sources feel they can meet the requirements, please contact the Contracting Officer by e-mail to caitlin.m.jenkins@cbp.dhs.gov by the response date and time listed with this notice. You must submit a capabilities statement displaying your ability to meet the requirements.

A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed action based upon responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government. Interested sources who submit data are responsible for appropriately marking information if it is proprietary in nature. The Government shall not reimburse interested sources for any information provided.

opps.title: INTENT TO AWARD TO A SINGLE SOURCE FORSTARBOARD MARITIME INTELLIGENCE WEBSITE
opps.description:
***The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s (NOAA) Acquisition & Grants Office (AGO), Management and Budget Acquisition Office (OMB), intends to negotiate, on a sole source basis, under authority of FAR 12.102(a) and 41 USC 190, with Starboard Maritime Intelligence Limited…. The requirement is for a web-based, satellite and terrestrial AIS data analysis application service capable of integrating with required regional fisheries management bodies (WCPFC/FFA) and providing high-frequency satellite AIS data for U.S. and foreign vessels, including consistent Class B AIS coverage for counter-IUU fishing operations.



***The sole source determination is based on NOAA’s requirement for a web-based, satellite and terrestrial AIS data analysis application service. Starboard Maritime Intelligence Limited is the only vendor that provides a platform capable of integrating with required regional fisheries management bodies (WCPFC/FFA) and providing high-frequency satellite AIS data for both U.S. and foreign vessels, including consistent Class B AIS coverage essential for counter-IUU fishing operations.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
As stated in the recently completed State Laws Digest for Illegal Passing of School Buses, the term "illegal passing" is a prevalent concept; however, the report illustrated its lack of universality. Variations in state laws dictate that passing a bus from the front on a divided highway may be illegal in some states but permissible under specific roadway conditions in others. What constitutes illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neighboring one. Given the high mobility of the U.S. population, the proximity of urban centers to multiple jurisdictions, and the significant disparities in the content and language of applicable Vehicle and Traffic Laws (VTL), confusion regarding the legality of actions surrounding a school bus is not unexpected.

The lack of clarity in drafting illegal passing provisions further compromises the ability of the typical motorist to understand these VTL requirements. Some jurisdictions have redrafted their amended provisions to include, among other things, the use of cameras. Thus, their updates present a mostly coherent, integrated statement of the law. Other jurisdictions have provisions that appear essentially sequential in design. New or changed provisions simply appear at the end of the existing VTL section, even if they logically relate to a topic much earlier in the material. This type of statutory construction is difficult to follow and can be misleading.

As recommended in the state laws digest, and in anticipation of future legislative interest, this project will focus more deeply on current illegal passing laws and develop a draft model state law to address incidents of illegal passing of stopped school buses. This report will also investigate the potential benefits and feasibility of the citation being deemed a moving violation.

This project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This project will provide if a model law would be n
